query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "locateanything: fast and high-quality vision-language grounding with p" computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (d) and (f) as a foundational review and canonical reference for the domain of vision-language grounding and object detection, which is the specific technical area of the user's "LocateAnything" follow-up. Although it is a review rather than a specific follow-up experiment, it establishes the state-of-the-art baseline and architectural context necessary for a literature review on extending vision-language grounding methods.
